part #1: multiple objects
usage: the small, sparkling crystal hovered delicately on the corner of a sturdy, ebony piano. it meets the criteria as there are multiple objects in the sentence to describe.

part #2: one or several adjectives
usage: in this example, "small" and "sparkling" serve as adjectives describing one of the objects (the crystal). it meets the criteria as there are one or more adjectives characterizing this object or an object(s) together.

part #3: spatial relationships
usage: the crystal hovered delicately on the corner of... this meets the criteria under spatial as it introduces how they're positioned or relative to each other in the scene or composition.

part #4: part #4: number
usage: to meet the criteria of having more than two object in composite phrase, the example above shows three objects. 

part #5: number

3. choose "painting" and "large canvas"

5. use "a large canvas" instead of "a large tv screen."
6. use the next step words as required to create a continuous story.
